input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues here:

## 1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime

`StaticResource` is resolved **once** at XAML load time. For runtime theme switching, use **`DynamicResource`**:

```xml
<!-- ❌ Won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✅ Will update when the dictionary changes -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap your merged dictionary at runtime:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(isDark ? new DarkTheme() : new LightTheme());
```

## 2. Activity restart on Android settings change

Android destroys and recreates the `Activity` when `uiMode` changes. To prevent that, add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to your `MainActivity`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    MainLauncher = true,
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
        | ConfigChanges.Orientation
        | ConfigChanges.UiMode       // ← add this
        | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
        | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
        | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

This tells Android your app handles the UI-mode change itself, so it won't restart the Activity. You can then listen for the system theme change via `Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged` and swap dictionaries accordingly.
data.reasoningText:
The user has two issues with their .NET MAUI Android app related to dark mode:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors, but `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updated. They need to use `DynamicResource` for runtime theme switching.

2. **App restarting on Android dark mode toggle**: When the user toggles dark mode in Android settings, the app restarts (Activity is recreated). This is because Android destroys and recreates the Activity on configuration changes like `uiMode`. They need to handle this in their Android configuration.


I'll provide the specific fixes they need to implement.
